title: 2026 Dividend Champions: Reliable Payouts in an Uncertain World
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
**Key Takeaways**

* This curated list of 22 Dividend Champions showcases companies with an average of 17 consecutive years of dividend increases, demonstrating exceptional financial discipline and shareholder loyalty as of February 15, 2026.
* The group’s 10-year annualized dividend growth averages 8.2%, reflecting strong compounding potential for long-term income investors amid moderating inflation and economic recovery.
* Recent quarterly dividend hikes average 5.1%, with yields spanning 1.7% to 3.3% and P/E ratios around 20.8, offering a balanced mix of income and growth at reasonable valuations.
* Spanning sectors from utilities and financials to healthcare and industrials, these firms highlight diversification opportunities, with standout performers like NextEra Energy boasting 32 years of increases.

In an era marked by geopolitical tensions, AI-driven disruptions, and shifting monetary policies, dividend-paying stocks remain a beacon of stability for investors. The table spotlights 22 Dividend Champions—companies that have raised dividends annually for at least 10 years—based on their latest announcements in early 2026. These firms not only weathered recent market volatility but continue to prioritize shareholder returns through consistent hikes, backed by robust balance sheets and strategic adaptations. From regulated utilities providing essential services to innovative exchanges capturing global trade flows, this group exemplifies resilience. Below, we profile each company in detail, weaving in their business overview, dividend history, and investment appeal.

**Ameren Corporation (**[**AEE**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/AEE/)**)**

Ameren Corporation (AEE), a prominent Midwestern utility giant, powers homes and businesses across Illinois and Missouri with a focus on clean energy transitions. With 13 consecutive years of dividend increases, Ameren recently upped its quarterly payout from $0.71 to $0.75, a solid 5.63% jump that underscores its commitment to reliable income. This move aligns with a 10-year annualized growth rate of 5.50%, delivering a current yield of 2.70% and trading at a P/E of 20.74—attractive for defensive portfolios. As Ameren ramps up renewable investments amid federal incentives, its regulated model ensures steady cash flows, making it a low-volatility anchor for retirees and conservative traders seeking inflation-hedging stability.

**CME Group Inc. (**[**CME**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/CME/)**)**

CME Group Inc. (CME), the world’s leading derivatives marketplace, facilitates trading in futures and options across commodities, equities, and interest rates, benefiting from heightened global volatility. The company has extended its dividend streak to 16 years, announcing a quarterly increase from $1.25 to $1.30—a 4.00% rise—coupled with a 10-year growth rate of 9.60%. Yielding 1.72% with a P/E of 25.4, CME’s payouts are supported by record trading volumes in 2025, driven by rate cut expectations and geopolitical risks. For growth-oriented income investors, CME offers exposure to financial innovation, with AI-enhanced platforms poised to capture more market share in a digitized trading landscape.

**Quest Diagnostics Inc. (**[**DGX**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/DGX/)**)**

Quest Diagnostics Inc. (DGX), a diagnostics powerhouse providing lab testing services to healthcare providers and patients nationwide, has navigated post-pandemic demand shifts with agility. Marking 15 straight years of dividend growth, it hiked its quarterly dividend from $0.80 to $0.86, reflecting a robust 7.45% increase and a 10-year annualized rate of 7.12%. At a 1.69% yield and 18.4 P/E, the stock appeals to value hunters in healthcare. Quest’s expansion into advanced genomics and telehealth integrations positions it for sustained earnings, offering retail investors a defensive play with upside from aging demographics and preventive medicine trends.

**Equinix Inc. (**[**EQIX**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/EQIX/)**)**

Equinix Inc. (EQIX), a global leader in data center real estate, powers the digital economy by hosting cloud and interconnection services for tech behemoths. Extending its 11-year dividend streak, Equinix increased its quarterly dividend from $4.69 to $5.16—a impressive 10.02% surge—with a 10.75% 10-year growth rate. Offering a 2.16% yield and 30.9 P/E, it trades at a premium reflective of its REIT status and AI-fueled demand boom. Investors eyeing the data explosion from generative AI will find Equinix indispensable, blending real estate stability with explosive growth in hyperscale infrastructure.

**Gilead Sciences Inc. (**[**GILD**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/GILD/)**)**

Gilead Sciences Inc. (GILD), a biopharma trailblazer specializing in antivirals and oncology therapies like Biktarvy, continues to innovate in high-need therapeutic areas. With 16 years of uninterrupted dividend growth, it raised its quarterly payout from $0.31 to $0.37, a 3.23% increase backed by a 4.30% 10-year rate. Yielding 3.29% at an 18.77 P/E, GILD offers value in biotech. As it advances HIV cures and liver disease treatments, Gilead’s pipeline diversification mitigates patent cliffs, making it a compelling choice for healthcare dividend seekers balancing yield and innovation risk.

**Great-West Lifeco Inc. (GWO)**

Great-West Lifeco Inc. (GWO), a Canadian life insurance and retirement services provider with a vast North American footprint, emphasizes long-term financial security for clients. Boasting 12 consecutive dividend increases, it lifted its quarterly payout from $0.61 to $0.67—a 9.84% jump—with a 6.40% 10-year growth average. At a 4.34% yield and 11.27 P/E, GWO screams value for income purists. Its diversified operations in pensions and annuities, bolstered by favorable demographics, ensure payout sustainability, appealing to cross-border investors favoring steady, undervalued financials.

**Jack Henry & Associates Inc. (**[**JKHY**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/JKHY/)**)**

Jack Henry & Associates Inc. (JKHY), a fintech solutions provider empowering community banks with core processing and digital banking tools, thrives on the digitization of financial services. With 21 years of dividend hikes, it announced a quarterly increase from $1.33 to $1.47—10.53% higher—supported by a 9.68% 10-year rate. Yielding 1.26% with a 24.79 P/E, JKHY suits tech-savvy dividend hunters. As cybersecurity and payment innovations accelerate, Jack Henry’s niche in underserved markets positions it for organic growth, blending stability with fintech tailwinds.

**Manulife Financial Corp. (**[**MFC**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/MFC/)**)**

Manulife Financial Corp. (MFC), a global life insurer with deep Asian and Canadian roots, delivers wealth management and insurance amid rising longevity trends. With 13 years of dividend growth, it boosted its quarterly payout from $0.44 to $0.48—9.09% increase—alongside a 9.19% 10-year rate. Yielding 3.87% with a 10.98 P/E, MFC is a bargain for international exposure. Leveraging Asia’s growth engine and prudent risk management, Manulife offers diversified income with currency-hedged appeal for global portfolios.

**Magna International Inc. (**[**MGA**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/MGA/)**)**

Magna International Inc. (MGA), an automotive supplier excelling in engineering assemblies and EV components, adapts swiftly to industry electrification. Extending 18 years of increases, Magna raised its quarterly dividend from $0.44 to $0.485—a 10.23% hike—with 8.97% 10-year growth. Yielding 3.87% at 10.98 P/E, MGA yields generously for autos. As ADAS and battery tech surge, Magna’s OEM partnerships ensure relevance, rewarding patient investors with cyclical recovery upside.

**NextEra Energy Inc. (**[**NEE**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/NEE/)**)**

NextEra Energy Inc. (NEE), the largest U.S. renewable energy producer and utility, leads the green revolution with wind, solar, and nuclear assets. With an enviable 32-year streak, it increased its quarterly dividend from $0.5665 to $0.6232—10.11% growth—boasting 11.40% 10-year annualized rate. Yielding 2.66% at 23.34 P/E, NEE combines yield with growth. Its aggressive clean energy expansion, fueled by IRA subsidies, cements its status as a must-own for sustainable investing, blending utility safety with renewable megatrends.

**EnPro Industries Inc. (**[**NPO**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/NPO/)**)**

EnPro Industries Inc. (NPO), a diversified industrial tech firm focused on sealing solutions and motion control for harsh environments, serves aerospace and energy sectors. With 24 years of raises, it hiked quarterly from $0.30 to $0.31—3.50%—with 4.80% 10-year growth. Yielding 2.67% at 40.75 P/E, NPO trades rich but earns it through margins. Post-acquisition integrations and aftermarket strength position it for industrial rebound, appealing to niche dividend collectors.

**NorthWestern Energy Group Inc. (**[**NWE**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/NWE/)**)**

NorthWestern Energy Group Inc. (NWE), a regulated utility delivering gas and electric to Montana and South Dakota, prioritizes customer reliability in rural markets. Boasting 22 consecutive increases, it raised quarterly from $0.66 to $0.67—1.52%—with 3.42% 10-year rate. Yielding 3.84% at 18.73 P/E, NWE offers solid value. Its hydro and transmission investments amid energy demand growth ensure payout confidence, ideal for regional income strategies.

**Principal Financial Group Inc. (**[**PFG**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/PFG/)**)**

Principal Financial Group Inc. (PFG), a retirement and asset management leader with global reach, helps savers navigate wealth accumulation. With 18 years of growth, it upped quarterly from $0.79 to $0.80—1.27%—supported by 7.46% 10-year average. Yielding 3.54% at 9.64 P/E, PFG is undervalued. Fee-based revenue stability and ESG fund inflows bolster its appeal for financial sector dividend plays.

**Prologis Inc. (**[**PLD**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/PLD/)**)**

Prologis Inc. (PLD), the preeminent logistics REIT owning warehouses worldwide, capitalizes on e-commerce and supply chain evolution. With 17 years of hikes, it increased quarterly from $1.04 to $1.20—15.38%—with 10.68% 10-year growth. Yielding 3.09% at 26.66 P/E, PLD yields well for REITs. Nearshoring and AI logistics demand drive its premium, making it essential for real estate income with growth kicker.

**Restaurant Brands International Inc. (**[**QSR**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/QSR/)**)**

Restaurant Brands International Inc. (QSR), parent to Burger King, Tim Hortons, and Popeyes, scales quick-service dining globally. Extending 15 years, it raised quarterly from $1.20 to $1.27—5.83%—with 8.10% 10-year rate. Yielding 3.18% at 15.03 P/E, QSR balances value and taste. Menu innovation and franchise expansion in emerging markets fuel resilient cash flows for consumer dividend fans.

**RenaissanceRe Holdings Ltd. (**[**RNR**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/RNRG/)**)**

RenaissanceRe Holdings Ltd. (RNR), a Bermuda-based reinsurance titan mitigating catastrophe risks, leverages data for superior underwriting. With 31 years of increases, it boosted quarterly with strong 18.68% 10-year growth. Yielding 0.39% at 16.39 P/E, RNR prioritizes growth over yield. Climate modeling AI enhances its edge, suiting sophisticated investors in specialty insurance.

**Toromont Industries Ltd. (TIH)**

Toromont Industries Ltd. (TIH), a Canadian Caterpillar dealer and industrial distributor, supports mining and construction with equipment solutions. With 37 years of raises, it increased quarterly from $0.52 to $0.56—7.69%—with 11.93% 10-year rate. Yielding 1.09% at 29.29 P/E, TIH grows steadily. Resource sector recovery and rental expansions make it a cross-border gem for industrial dividends.

**T. Rowe Price Group Inc. (**[**TROW**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/TROW/)**)**

T. Rowe Price Group Inc. (TROW), a venerable asset manager renowned for active equity strategies, navigates market cycles with disciplined research. With 40 years of growth—the longest here—it hiked quarterly from $1.27 to $1.35—6.30%—with 9.34% 10-year average. Yielding 5.56% at 28.23 P/E, TROW yields richly. AUM inflows from retirement accounts sustain payouts, ideal for mutual fund enthusiasts.

**Tractor Supply Company (**[**TSCO**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/TSCO/)**)**

Tractor Supply Company (TSCO), the go-to rural lifestyle retailer for farming and pet supplies, taps America’s heartland consumer boom. With 26 years of increases, it raised quarterly from $0.83 to $0.85—2.41%—with 15.73% 10-year growth. Yielding 1.49% at 23.59 P/E, TSCO blends retail resilience with expansion. E-commerce and store growth in ex-urban areas drive its appeal for consumer cyclical income.

**UFP Industries Inc. (**[**UFPI**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/UFPI/)**)**

UFP Industries Inc. (UFPI), a wood products manufacturer supplying retail and construction markets, innovates in sustainable building materials. With 14 years of hikes, it upped quarterly from $0.35 to $0.36—2.86%—with 17.75% 10-year rate. Yielding 1.26% at 21.48 P/E, UFPI offers growth tilt. Housing recovery and retail packaging demand position it for lumber cycle upturns.

**Vulcan Materials Company (**[**VMC**](https://tickeron.com/ticker/VMC/)**)**

Vulcan Materials Company (VMC), the top U.S. aggregates producer for infrastructure and construction, fuels America’s build-back efforts. With 13 consecutive increases, it boosted quarterly from $0.49 to $0.53—6.12%—with 11.21% 10-year growth. Yielding 1.34% at 36.36 P/E, VMC trades premium on infra tailwinds. IIJA funding and urbanization trends ensure long-term volume, making it a infrastructure dividend powerhouse.

[**Tickeron’s AI Trading Bots**](https://tickeron.com/app/ai-robots/virtualagents/top-10/)**: Automating Dividend Champion Strategies**

Retail traders who want to build, monitor, and grow a portfolio of these Dividend Champions without spending hours glued to screens now have a powerful ally in [Tickeron’s AI trading bots](https://tickeron.com/app/ai-robots/virtualagents/top-10/). The platform’s intelligent agents, including the adaptive “Double Agent” model, scan real-time dividend announcements, payout ratios, earnings quality, and sector momentum across all 22 names in the table. They automatically flag fresh hikes (like Prologis’ 15.38% jump or NextEra’s 10.11% raise), calculate sustainable yield-on-cost trajectories, and execute buys or rebalances in high-conviction names such as NEE, TROW, or PLD. Whether you’re dollar-cost averaging into a new position or harvesting gains on a 40-year streak like T. Rowe Price, Tickeron’s bots handle the heavy lifting 24/7, turning the steady compounding power of Dividend Champions into a truly hands-free income machine for everyday investors.
